comment: Unbeatable location, you can walk to Shibuya Crossing in two minutes. Spotless.
Kenji was a wonderful host, super responsive and left great neighborhood tips.
Quiet despite being in Shinjuku, and the park nearby is gorgeous in spring.
Lovely apartment, a bit of street noise at night but easy subway access.
Staying in a real machiya in Gion was the highlight of our trip. So peaceful.
Comfortable room near Senso-ji, shared kitchen was tidy and well stocked.
Steps from the bamboo grove, woke up early to beat the crowds. Tatami was cozy.
Traditional feel and walkable to Senso-ji. Haruto checked in to make sure we settled.
Ryokan vibes without the price tag. Kiyomizu-dera at sunset was a short walk away.
The private garden made mornings perfect. Akiko thought of every detail.
Tucked into a quiet Shibuya backstreet but close to everything. Loved the loft layout.
Compact but smartly designed. Great base for exploring Shinjuku at night.
A minute from Nishiki Market. Quiet at night and the machiya was beautifully kept.
Tiny but exactly what I needed near Tokyo Station for an early Shinkansen.
Drove out from Seattle for a quiet weekend. Riverside deck and easy trail access were perfect.
Such a cozy cabin. Leavenworth in winter felt like a postcard. Emma was a great host.
Great little Portland loft for a weekend off. Walkable to coffee and bookstores.
Woke up to water views every morning. Friday Harbor was the reset I needed.
Quiet forest cabin a short drive from the Rainier trailheads. No cell signal, exactly the point.
Perfect basecamp for a Mount Rainier weekend. Wood stove kept us warm at night.
Charming A-frame, loft bedroom was a fun touch. A little cozy for four but worth it.
Loved the Pearl District location. Big windows and a comfy bed after a long day.
Orcas Island is magic and this cabin made it easy. Quick walk to the ferry.
Simple mountain studio in Packwood, great value and an easy drive to Rainier.
Riverside walks along the Sumida every morning. Asakusa is underrated as a home base.
